'''''Star Wars: Droids''''' was an eight-issue comic book series published by [[Star Comics]], an imprint of [[Marvel Comics]], from [[1986]] to [[1987]]. The series was based on the animated [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] television show, which aired in the 1985–86 season and was canceled shortly after this series began.

While the TV show was set around [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], this series and ended with a semi-adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] told from [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO's]] point of view. However, the blog series [[The Droids Re-Animated]] officially stated that issues 1–5 took place before the animated series. This chronology explains the second issue of the comic depicting the characters [[Tig Fromm]] and [[Vlix Oncard]], who had been turned over to [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] in the last episode of the first TV story arc. It also explains a reference to the comic series character [[Zell]] in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' that takes place before the description of the events of the TV show.

In the 1990s, [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] released a [[Star Wars: Droids (1994)|''Star Wars: Droids'' (1994)]].
